page: Vultr vhp-4c-12gb-amd is a High Performance family virtual machine featuring 4 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor. The system operates on the x86_64 architecture with a clock speed of 2.0 GHz, 16 MB of L3 cache, and 12.0 GB of system memory, yielding 3.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled with 260 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present on this configuration.

Performance benchmarks reveal a mixed profile. The AMD EPYC-Rome processor demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core performance and PassMark CPU scores fall into the average range. Memory performance is a notable bottleneck; while cached small-block read bandwidth is average at 380.64 GB/sec, uncached large-block read and write speeds are weak, and the PassMark memory score is poor, placing in the bottom 10%. Data decompression and database operations also score poorly, with PassMark database operations and bzip3 decompression falling into the bottom 10%.

Because this server utilizes shared CPU allocation, it offers a cost-effective option for workloads that do not require sustained, dedicated compute resources. The combination of strong single-core performance and a 260 GB NVMe SSD makes it suitable for lightweight web serving, development environments, and basic utility applications. However, the weak memory bandwidth and poor database operation benchmarks suggest it is less optimal for high-throughput database hosting, heavy ETL pipelines, or memory-intensive applications.
description: The Vultr vhp-4c-12gb-amd is a High Performance virtual server configured with 4 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 12.0 GB of RAM, and 260 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this instance delivers strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% in stress-ng benchmarks. However, its shared CPU allocation and weak uncached memory bandwidth present performance tradeoffs, particularly for memory-intensive tasks. PassMark database operations and bzip3 decompression benchmarks fall into the bottom 10%, indicating limitations for heavy data processing. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring a balance of solid single-core compute and fast local storage without the premium of dedicated resources. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, lightweight web serving, and development environments.
og_description: Vultr vhp-4c-12gb-amd is a High Performance server with 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 12 GB RAM, and 260 GB NVMe SSD, offering strong single-core performance alongside budget-friendly shared resources.
meta_description: Vultr vhp-4c-12gb-amd features 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 12 GB RAM, and 260 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core performance for budget workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server combining strong single-core AMD EPYC performance with fast NVMe storage for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 4 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs with 12.0 GB of system memory
260 GB of local NVMe SSD storage included
Strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Best suited for lightweight web serving and development environments
